perturbation.original_text: 2. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. All information disclosed by or on behalf of a party (“Discloser”) and/or its Affiliates during the term of this Agreement which is received, learned, or observed by the other party (“Recipient”) and/or its Affiliates is “Confidential Information,” including, but not limited to, business and financial information, marketing and strategic plans, customer lists, employee lists, reports, technologies, proc esses and operations, compilations, analyses, forecasts, studies, summaries, notes, data, product designs, drawings, prints, specifications, production schedules, test data, operations, inventions, know -how, component and part designs, schematics, formulat ions, samples, passwords, machines, network IDs, prototypes, mock -ups, templates, processing and control information, product performance data, manuals, purchase and sales records, warranty and repair records, marketing information, quotes and pricing info rmation, computer programs, or the like, including e- mail and electronic communication discussing any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. PRIVATE DETAILS. All information disclosed by or on behalf of a party (“Discloser”) and/or its Affiliates during the term of this Agreement which is received, learned, or observed by the other party (“Recipient”) and/or its Affiliates is “Private Details,” including, but not limited to, business and financial information, marketing and strategic plans, customer lists, employee lists, reports, technologies, proc esses and operations, compilations, analyses, forecasts, studies, summaries, notes, data, product designs, drawings, prints, specifications, production schedules, test data, operations, inventions, know -how, component and part designs, schematics, formulat ions, samples, passwords, machines, network IDs, prototypes, mock -ups, templates, processing and control information, product performance data, manuals, purchase and sales records, warranty and repair records, marketing information, quotes and pricing info rmation, computer programs, or the like, including e- mail and electronic communication discussing any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Details' in the definition section, while the rest of the contract refers to 'Confidential Information', leads to an in-text contradiction. The obligations regarding non-disclosure and non-use (Sections 3 and 4) become ambiguous because they refer to something that is no longer the defined term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. NON -DISCLOSURE. Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other person or entity. Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any of its Representatives or Representatives of its Affiliates, except as required to carry out the Purpose under this Agreement for the benefit of Deere and its Affiliates. “Representative” shall mean any officer, director, employee, contractor, agent, supplier, or advisor of its respective entity. “Affiliate” shall mean any corporation, partnership, or other entity that, either directly or indirectly, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with a party, where control is defined as having more than a fifty percent (50%) controlling inter est.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Details' creates a direct conflict with Section 3, which explicitly references 'Confidential Information'. Because the contract now uses the term 'Confidential Information' in Section 3 and 'Private Details' in the modified definition, and they are not defined as the same, there is an in-text contradiction, as the term in Section 3 no longer has a clear definition. This is because the explanation explicitly states that the rest of the contract refers to 'Confidential Information'. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition, which states that an in-text contradiction occurs when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 5. CONFIDENTIALITY. Recipient shall take reasonable measures to protect Confidential Information from misuse and unauthorized disclosure, but in no event less than the measures it takes to protect its own confidential information. Prior to disclosure of Confidential Information to Recipient’s contractor, agent, supplier or advisor, Recipient shall ensure that such contractor, agent, supplier or advisor has executed a non -disclosure a greement for the benefit of Green Industrial Supply, Inc. in form and substance equivalent to this Agreement. Recipient shall be responsible for any misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by its Representatives or Representatives of its Affiliates. A disclosure of Confidential Information required under legal process shall not be considered to be a breach of confidentiality by Recipient or a waiver of such obligation by Discloser. Before any such disclosure, however, Recipient shall provide prompt written notice to Discloser to enable it to seek a protective order or prevent disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. PROTECTION. Recipient shall take reasonable measures to protect Private Data from misuse and unauthorized disclosure, but in no event less than the measures it takes to protect its own confidential information. Prior to disclosure of Private Data to Recipient’s contractor, agent, supplier or advisor, Recipient shall ensure that such contractor, agent, supplier or advisor has executed a non -disclosure a greement for the benefit of Green Industrial Supply, Inc. in form and substance equivalent to this Agreement. Recipient shall be responsible for any misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Private Data by its Representatives or Representatives of its Affiliates. A disclosure of Private Data required under legal process shall not be considered to be a breach of confidentiality by Recipient or a waiver of such obligation by Discloser. Before any such disclosure, however, Recipient shall provide prompt written notice to Discloser to enable it to seek a protective order or prevent disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidentiality' to 'Protection' and 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Data' in Section 5 creates contradiction because the agreement then refers to ‘confidential information’ in the phrase ‘measures it takes to protect its own confidential information’. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty about which information is subject to the protection measures, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. PROTECTION. Recipient shall take reasonable measures to protect Private Data from misuse and unauthorized disclosure, but in no event less than the measures it takes to protect its own confidential information. Prior to disclosure of Private Data to Recipient’s contractor, agent, supplier or advisor, Recipient shall ensure that such contractor, agent, supplier or advisor has executed a non -disclosure a greement for the benefit of Green Industrial Supply, Inc. in form and substance equivalent to this Agreement. Recipient shall be responsible for any misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Private Data by its Representatives or Representatives of its Affiliates. A disclosure of Private Data required under legal process shall not be considered to be a breach of confidentiality by Recipient or a waiver of such obligation by Discloser. Before any such disclosure, however, Recipient shall provide prompt written notice to Discloser to enable it to seek a protective order or prevent disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Data' in Section 5, while retaining the phrase 'measures it takes to protect its own confidential information'. This creates a direct contradiction because the agreement now refers to both 'Private Data' and 'confidential information', leading to ambiguity and uncertainty about which information is subject to the protection measures. The modified text directly conflicts with itself, creating an in-text contradiction as described in the Perturbation Explanation and falling under the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition. The Changed Text and Contradicted Text are essentially the same, but that is because the Changed Text itself contains the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. RETURN OF MATERIALS. All forms of Confidential Information, tangible and electronic, and all copies thereof, shall be and remain the sole and exclusive property of Discloser, and shall be promptly returned or destroyed by Recipient upon the earlier of the written request of Discloser or the termination of this Agreement. Except as noted in the immediately following sentence, Confidential Information contained in electronically archived notes, reports, correspondences, emails, and other business records prepared by Recipient in connection with the Purpose under this Agreement need not be returned or destroyed, but shall be subject to all other terms of this Agreement. Confidential Information in the form of schematics, prints, drawings, formulations, and descriptions of processes and know -how, and the like, must be returned to Discloser or destroyed pursuant to this Section 6.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. RETURN OF ASSETS. All forms of Confidential Information, tangible and electronic, and all copies thereof, shall be and remain the sole and exclusive property of Discloser, and shall be promptly returned or destroyed by Recipient upon the earlier of the written request of Discloser or the termination of this Agreement. Except as noted in the immediately following sentence, Business Data contained in electronically archived notes, reports, correspondences, emails, and other business records prepared by Recipient in connection with the Purpose under this Agreement need not be returned or destroyed, but shall be subject to all other terms of this Agreement. Confidential Information in the form of schematics, prints, drawings, formulations, and descriptions of processes and know -how, and the like, must be returned to Discloser or destroyed pursuant to this Section 6.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Business Data' in the second sentence of Section 6 (Return of Materials) introduces a contradiction. The first and third sentences of the section still refer to 'Confidential Information', while the second references ‘Business Data’. Given that the term 'Business Data' is not defined anywhere else in the agreement, it's unclear whether it is a subset of, or equivalent to, 'Confidential Information', or if it's a new term altogether, leading to uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. RETURN OF ASSETS. All forms of Confidential Information, tangible and electronic, and all copies thereof, shall be and remain the sole and exclusive property of Discloser, and shall be promptly returned or destroyed by Recipient upon the earlier of the written request of Discloser or the termination of this Agreement. Except as noted in the immediately following sentence, Business Data contained in electronically archived notes, reports, correspondences, emails, and other business records prepared by Recipient in connection with the Purpose under this Agreement need not be returned or destroyed, but shall be subject to all other terms of this Agreement. Confidential Information in the form of schematics, prints, drawings, formulations, and descriptions of processes and know -how, and the like, must be returned to Discloser or destroyed pursuant to this Section 6.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'Confidential Information' to 'Business Data' in the second sentence of Section 6. The first and third sentences of the section still refer to 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction, as it's unclear whether 'Business Data' is a subset of, equivalent to, or distinct from 'Confidential Information'. Since 'Business Data' is not defined, it's unclear if obligations for one apply to the other, satisfying the condition for misaligned terminology that results in an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION UNDER THIS AGREEMENT IS PROVIDED TO RECIPIENT “AS- IS” WITHOUT ANY REPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, AS TO ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE. Both parties represent and warrant they will comply with all applicable federal, state and local laws, rules, regulations, statutes, ordinances , codes,
perturbation.changed_text: 8. REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES. ALL Protected Details UNDER THIS AGREEMENT IS PROVIDED TO RECIPIENT “AS- IS” WITHOUT ANY REPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, AS TO ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE. Both parties represent and warrant they will comply with all applicable federal, state and local laws, rules, regulations, statutes, ordinances , codes,
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the term “Confidential Information” to “Protected Details” creates contradiction because the original definition of the Confidential Information is not reflecting the new change, in addition in other section of the NDA where “Confidential Information” is being reffered, it creates ambiguity with the new term and it becomes uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. All information disclosed by or on behalf of a party (“Discloser”) and/or its Affiliates during the term of this Agreement which is received, learned, or observed by the other party (“Recipient”) and/or its Affiliates is “Confidential Information,” including, but not limited to, business and financial information, marketing and strategic plans, customer lists, employee lists, reports, technologies, proc esses and operations, compilations, analyses, forecasts, studies, summaries, notes, data, product designs, drawings, prints, specifications, production schedules, test data, operations, inventions, know -how, component and part designs, schematics, formulat ions, samples, passwords, machines, network IDs, prototypes, mock -ups, templates, processing and control information, product performance data, manuals, purchase and sales records, warranty and repair records, marketing information, quotes and pricing info rmation, computer programs, or the like, including e- mail and electronic communication discussing any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Information" to "Protected Details" in Section 8 creates a contradiction with Section 2, which defines "Confidential Information." Section 2 does not define "Protected Details," making it unclear what information is subject to the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 2. This directly contradicts the existing definition and creates ambiguity where there was certainty, causing an in-text contradiction as per the "Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction" definition.
